notes: One LIVE aider REPL session that finishes a turn, sits idle PAST readyTTL while its process stays alive, then resumes normally on the next prompt — ready→working→ready, a long idle dwell in ready, then ready→working→ready again, on a SINGLE live entity (one proc-<PID>). WHY this proves the scenario: aider's PID is discoverable via the /aider command-line match (pid.go), so the daemon's readyTTL sweep (pid_manager.go) first filters stale ready sessions, then explicitly SKIPS deletion when kill(pid,0)==nil — the live aider TUI keeps the ready session out of the sweep exactly as it does for claudecode. The recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s on the daemon so the idle_window's 15000ms duration_at_least_ms cleanly exceeds the TTL in ~45s instead of the 30-min production default; the shortened TTL only affects the recording, not production behaviour. If the liveness skip regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (transcript_removed for the live session during idle, or a flicker off ready). WHY interactive (not headless `aider --message`): headless aider is single-shot and exits after one reply, so the process would be DEAD during the idle window — the very condition the scenario must exclude. The interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh) keeps ONE aider TUI alive via tmux across both sends and the idle sleep, killing the tmux session only at the very end → the process is genuinely alive throughout the dwell. AIDER TURN-END IS SYNTHESIZED, NOT MARKED: the markdown transcript (.aider.chat.history.md, FilesUnderCWD) has no turn-end marker; a `> Tokens:` line closes one model CALL, and end-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms) quiet, clamped by the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll, so the EFFECTIVE working→ready latency after a turn's last line is ~5s. The recipe budgets a ~5s post-turn buffer plus a 20s idle sleep so first_turn_end settles before the dwell window is measured and the 20s sleep clearly exceeds the 15s TTL. LAZY TRANSCRIPT: .aider.chat.history.md materializes only once the first user input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) BEFORE the per-cwd transcript appears — session_birth (asserting only 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch') legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN/IDLE arcs must all land on the session that goes working: first_turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (the pre-session never enters working); every later phase, the pid_bind, and the idle_window then pin to first_turn_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance). Stable structurally: ready→working→ready, a ≥15s ready dwell with no removal or flicker, then ready→working→ready again on the same session, final ready. NOTE: this spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET — recording is deferred until aider auth is available in-env (no provider key present at authoring time; headless aider hangs on OpenRouter OAuth).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the lazy transcript has not materialized yet)
First user prompt → working (binds the live aider session; the pre-session never enters working)
PID bound to the live aider session — the binding the readyTTL liveness check polls
First turn completes back to ready (IdleFlush synthesizes turn-end after the prompt goes quiet); state never entered waiting
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL while its process stays alive; state remains ready throughout the idle window (no removal, no flicker to working or waiting)
Next user prompt after the long idle: ready → working resumes normally on the same live session
Second turn completes back to ready; final session state is ready
